Question: How do I enroll in a dental plan?
Answer: Enrolling is straightforward and I am happy to walk you through it. Log in to the member portal, open the Benefits tab, choose Dental Plans, compare the two available options side by side, and click Enroll on the one that fits your needs. Coverage starts on the first day of next month and you will get a confirmation email right away.

Question: Can I add my spouse to my coverage?
Answer: Absolutely, and it is a common request. You can add a spouse during open enrollment or within 30 days of a qualifying life event such as marriage. In the portal go to Family Coverage, select Add Dependent, upload the marriage certificate, and the change usually processes within two business days. I can also point you to the required documents list if that helps.

Question: What does my plan cover for physiotherapy?
Answer: Great question and the details matter here. Your plan covers up to 20 outpatient physiotherapy sessions per calendar year when prescribed by a physician. There is a 20 dollar copay per session, no deductible applies, and home-visit physiotherapy is covered at 50 percent for members recovering from surgery. Would you like the referral form link?

Question: How do I get a replacement insurance card?
Answer: Happy to help with that. You can order a replacement card in under a minute: portal, Profile, Order Replacement Card, and it ships in 5 to 7 business days. Meanwhile you can download a digital card immediately from the same page and use it at any provider, it is fully valid.

Question: Is mental health counseling covered?
Answer: Yes, and I want to make sure you get the full picture. Outpatient counseling is covered with a 20 dollar copay per session with no annual session limit. Teletherapy through our partner network carries no copay at all. If you need help finding an in-network counselor I can explain how to filter the provider directory by specialty and language.

Question: What is my deductible status this year?
Answer: Let me explain how to check that precisely. Your portal dashboard shows Deductible Progress right on the home screen, including how much you have paid toward the individual and family deductibles and which claims counted. It refreshes within 48 hours of each claim processing, so it is the most current source available.

Question: How do I submit an out-of-network claim?
Answer: There are three simple steps and I will spell them out. First download the out-of-network claim form from the Documents page. Second attach the itemized bill and proof of payment from your provider. Third upload everything under Claims then Submit New Claim. Reimbursement usually arrives within 15 business days and you can track status in real time.

Question: Can I change my primary care physician?
Answer: Of course, and you can do it any time, not just during enrollment. Open the portal, go to My Care Team, tap Change PCP, and search by name, location, or language. The change takes effect the first of the following month, and your new PCP's office is notified automatically so your records transfer without any action from you.

Question: Does the plan cover prescription glasses?
Answer: Yes, through the vision rider, and here are the specifics so there are no surprises. You get an allowance of 150 dollars toward frames or contact lenses every 12 months plus one covered eye exam per year. Participating optical stores apply the allowance instantly at checkout; elsewhere you pay and claim reimbursement with the receipt.

Question: How do I set up autopay for premiums?
Answer: Setting up autopay takes about two minutes and saves you from late fees entirely. In the portal open Billing, choose Autopay, pick a bank account or card, and select the payment date that suits you, either the 1st or the 15th. You will receive a reminder email three days before each charge, and you can pause or cancel anytime.

Question: What vaccinations are free under my plan?
Answer: All ACIP-recommended vaccines are covered at 100 percent as preventive care, which means zero cost to you at in-network pharmacies and clinics. That includes the annual flu shot, COVID boosters, shingles for eligible ages, Tdap, and travel vaccines when medically indicated. Walk-ins work at most network pharmacies, no appointment needed.

Question: How can I estimate costs before a procedure?
Answer: There is a purpose-built tool for exactly this and I recommend it warmly. The Cost Estimator under Tools lets you search any procedure code or plain-English name and shows the negotiated price at each nearby facility, your expected out-of-pocket after deductible, and quality ratings side by side. It is accurate to within about 10 percent for most procedures.
